title: Andersonia axilliflora

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as respiratory issues and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Andersonia axilliflora include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications, such as salves and ointments, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
